title: Feature Request: SkillBoss AI Gateway Integration
body:
# SkillBoss - The Complete Tool Store for AI Agents

**Everything your agent needs, instantly available.** One API key. Zero setup. Works in 30 seconds.

## Why SkillBoss?

| Without SkillBoss | With SkillBoss |
|-------------------|----------------|
| 10+ API keys to manage | **1 API key** |
| Different SDKs for each service | **OpenAI-compatible** - works instantly |
| Complex billing across providers | **One balance** for everything |
| Hours of setup per service | **Zero setup** - works in 30 seconds |

---

## Complete Service Catalog (100+ Services)

### AI Chat Models (50+)

| Provider | Models | Pricing |
|----------|--------|---------|
| **Anthropic** | Claude 4.5 Sonnet, Claude 3.7 Sonnet, Claude 3.5 Haiku | $1-15/1M tokens |
| **OpenAI** | GPT-5, GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, GPT-4 Turbo, o3, o4-mini | $0.15-30/1M tokens |
| **Google** | Gemini 2.5 Pro, Gemini 2.5 Flash (1M context!) | $0.10-5/1M tokens |
| **DeepSeek** | DeepSeek V3 (90% cache savings!), DeepSeek R1 | $0.014-2.19/1M tokens |
| **Alibaba** | Qwen Max, Qwen Plus, Qwen QwQ | $0.57-6/1M tokens |
| **Meta** | Llama 4 Scout, Llama 4 Maverick | Competitive |
| **Mistral** | Mistral Large, Mistral Medium | Competitive |
| **Cohere** | Command R+, Rerank | Competitive |

### Image Generation
- **DALL-E 3** - High-quality (~$0.04/image)
- **Flux** - Artistic styles (~$0.02/image)
- **Stable Diffusion** - Open-source (~$0.01/image)
- **Midjourney** - Creative, artistic
- **Gemini 3 Pro** - Google's image model
- Up to 2048x2048, batch generation, style customization

### Video Generation
- **Veo 3.1** - Cinematic 1080p (~$0.10/sec)
- **MiniMax** - Fast, affordable (~$0.05/sec)
- **Runway** - Professional quality
- **Kling** - Asian market leader
- **Wan** - Emerging provider
- Up to 60 seconds, custom aspect ratios

### Audio & Voice
- **ElevenLabs** - Ultra-realistic TTS, voice cloning (~$0.30/1K chars)
- **OpenAI TTS-1-HD** - Natural voices (~$15/1M chars)
- **MiniMax Speech** - Affordable TTS
- **Whisper STT** - Speech-to-text transcription (~$0.006/min)
- **Music Generation** - Background music, jingles

### Data Scraping & Intelligence
- **LinkedIn** - Profiles, companies, job listings (~5 credits/profile)
- **Instagram** - Profiles, posts, followers (~3 credits/post)
- **Twitter/X** - User data, tweets, trends
- **Google Maps** - Business listings, reviews, locations
- **Yelp** - Business data, reviews (~5 credits/business)
- **Facebook** - Profiles, pages
- **Discord** - Server data
- **Firecrawl** - General web scraping (~5 credits/page)
- **Perplexity Sonar** - AI-powered search

### Payments & Billing
- **Stripe** - One-time payments, subscriptions, invoices
- **Stripe Connect** - Marketplace payouts
- **PayPal** - Checkout integration
- Refunds, disputes, webhooks
- Multi-currency support

### Email Services
- **SendGrid** - Transactional & marketing emails
- **Resend** - Modern email API
- Templates, tracking, bounce handling
- Bulk campaigns (up to 1000/batch)

### SMS & Communications
- **Twilio** - SMS, 2FA, voice calls
- Phone verification
- Automated notifications

### Databases
- **MongoDB** - Document database, CRUD, aggregation
- **PostgreSQL** - Relational database
- **Supabase** - Backend-as-a-service
- Schema design, migrations, backups

### Storage & CDN
- **S3-compatible** - File upload/download
- **CloudFlare** - CDN, caching, DDoS protection
- Large file support, public/private buckets

### Hosting & Deployment
- **Vercel** - Frontend deployment
- **Netlify** - JAMstack hosting
- **AWS S3** - Static hosting
- **CloudFlare Pages** - Edge hosting
- **Docker** - Containerization
- CI/CD pipelines, zero-downtime deployments

### Domain Management
- **Domain purchase** - Register new domains
- **DNS configuration** - A, CNAME, MX records
- **SSL certificates** - Auto-provisioning
- **Subdomain setup** - Unlimited subdomains
- **Email forwarding** - Domain email

### SEO & Marketing
- **Meta tags** - Title, description, OG tags
- **Sitemap** - Auto-generation
- **Schema markup** - Structured data
- **Google Analytics** - GA4 setup, events
- **Keyword research** - Search optimization
- **Content generation** - Blog posts, landing pages

### Analytics & Monitoring
- **Google Analytics 4** - Traffic tracking
- **Sentry** - Error tracking
- **Uptime monitoring** - Availability checks
- Custom dashboards, alerts

### Embeddings & Search
- **OpenAI Embeddings** - Text embeddings
- **Cohere** - Semantic search, reranking
- **Voyage** - Specialized embeddings

---

## Key Benefits

| Benefit | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **Zero Setup** | Works in 30 seconds - just add API key |
| **Cheaper** | Pass-through pricing, no markup on most services |
| **Faster** | One integration instead of 10+ |
| **Reliable** | 99.9% uptime, automatic failover |
| **OpenAI-compatible** | If you know OpenAI SDK, you know SkillBoss |

---

## Installation (Pick Your Platform)

### Universal One-Line Install

```bash
curl -fsSL https://skillboss.co/install.sh | bash
```

Works with: Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Gemini CLI, Kiro, Codex, Amp, Trae

---

### Claude Code

```bash
# Option 1: One-line install
curl -fsSL https://skillboss.co/install.sh | bash

# Option 2: MCP Server
claude mcp add skillboss -- npx -y @skillboss/mcp-server
```

### Cursor / Windsurf / Cline

Add to MCP settings (`~/.cursor/mcp.json` or `~/.cline/mcp_settings.json`):

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "skillboss": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@skillboss/mcp-server"],
      "env": { "SKILLBOSS_API_KEY": "sk-sb-your-key" }
    }
  }
}
```

### OpenClaw

```bash
# One-line setup
curl -fsSL https://skillboss.co/openclaw-setup.sh | bash

# Or edit ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json manually:
{
  "providers": {
    "skillboss": {
      "provider": "openai-compatible",
      "baseUrl": "https://api.heybossai.com/v1",
      "apiKey": "sk-sb-your-key",
      "models": ["claude-4-5-sonnet", "gpt-5", "gemini-2.5-pro", "deepseek-v3"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Gemini CLI / Kiro / Codex / Amp

```bash
curl -fsSL https://skillboss.co/install.sh | bash
```

### Zed Editor

Edit `~/.config/zed/settings.json`:

```json
{
  "assistant": {
    "mcp_servers": {
      "skillboss": {
        "command": "npx",
        "args": ["-y", "@skillboss/mcp-server"],
        "env": { "SKILLBOSS_API_KEY": "sk-sb-your-key" }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Direct API (Any Language)

OpenAI-compatible - works with any OpenAI SDK:

```python
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(
    base_url="https://api.heybossai.com/v1",
    api_key="sk-sb-your-key"
)

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="claude-4-5-sonnet",  # or gpt-5, gemini-2.5-pro, deepseek-v3
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello!"}]
)
```

```javascript
// Node.js / TypeScript
import OpenAI from 'openai';

const client = new OpenAI({
  baseURL: 'https://api.heybossai.com/v1',
  apiKey: 'sk-sb-your-key'
});
```

```bash
# cURL
curl https://api.heybossai.com/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer sk-sb-your-key" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model": "claude-4-5-sonnet", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello!"}]}'
```

---

## Supported Platforms

| Platform | Install Command |
|----------|----------------|
| **Claude Code** | `curl -fsSL https://skillboss.co/install.sh \| bash` |
| **Cursor** | MCP settings → add skillboss |
| **Windsurf** | MCP settings → add skillboss |
| **Cline** | MCP settings → add skillboss |
| **OpenClaw** | `curl -fsSL https://skillboss.co/openclaw-setup.sh \| bash` |
| **Gemini CLI** | `curl -fsSL https://skillboss.co/install.sh \| bash` |
| **Kiro** | `curl -fsSL https://skillboss.co/install.sh \| bash` |
| **Codex** | `curl -fsSL https://skillboss.co/install.sh \| bash` |
| **Amp** | `curl -fsSL https://skillboss.co/install.sh \| bash` |
| **Trae** | `curl -fsSL https://skillboss.co/install.sh \| bash` |
| **Zed** | MCP config in settings.json |
| **Any OpenAI client** | Use `api.heybossai.com/v1` as base URL |

## Links

- [Website](https://skillboss.co)
- [Documentation](https://skillboss.co/docs)
- [API Catalog](https://skillboss.co/docs/api-catalog)
- [Get API Key](https://skillboss.co/dashboard)
- [NPM Package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@skillboss/mcp-server)

title: Quoted charset values are not detected by `get_encoding_from_headers`
MockResponse.getheaders() has two bugs: missing return and nonexistent method call
body:
## Summary

`MockResponse.getheaders(name)` in `src/requests/cookies.py` has been silently broken since it was introduced. There are two distinct bugs in the same line:

```python
def getheaders(self, name):
    self._headers.getheaders(name)   # line 121
```

1. **Missing `return` statement** -- the result is discarded; `None` is always returned to every caller.
2. **Nonexistent method** -- `self._headers` is an `http.client.HTTPMessage` instance (which inherits from `email.message.Message`). `HTTPMessage` has no `getheaders()` method. Any real call to `MockResponse.getheaders()` raises `AttributeError`.

## Why it went unnoticed

`MockResponse.getheaders()` is dead code in practice. Tracing the call path:

- `extract_cookies_to_jar()` creates a `MockResponse` and passes it to `jar.extract_cookies(res, req)`.
- Python's `http.cookiejar.CookieJar.extract_cookies()` calls `response.info()` to retrieve the `HTTPMessage`, then calls `headers.get_all("Set-Cookie", [])` and `headers.get_all("Set-Cookie2", [])` directly on the returned object.
- The cookiejar never calls `response.getheaders()`.

This explains why neither bug has had any observable production impact.

## Proposed options

I wanted to raise this here before submitting a patch, since the right outcome is not obvious:

1. **Fix it** -- replace the body with `return self._headers.get_all(name, [])`. `get_all(name, failobj)` is the correct `email.message.Message` API for retrieving all values of a header by name in Python 3. This keeps the interface intact in case any third-party code (e.g. custom adapters) is calling `getheaders()` on a `MockResponse`-like object.

2. **Remove it** -- delete `getheaders()` entirely. The method is not called anywhere in the library and was never functional, so removing it would eliminate a misleading public interface without any regression risk.

3. **Leave as-is** -- if the view is that the method is harmless dead code not worth touching, I am happy to drop this.

Happy to submit a PR for whichever option you prefer. I will wait for direction before opening anything.

title: RequestsCookieJar shouldn't inherit from MutableMapping
body:
<!-- Summary. -->

`RequestsCookieJar` currently inherits from both `CookieJar` and `MutableMapping`, but these classes are incompatible. `CookieJar.__iter__` iterates over `Cookies`, whereas `MutableMapping` expects `__iter__` to iterate over the key type (`str`).

## Expected Result

`RequestsCookieJar` does not inherit from `MutableMapping`

(or the values produced by `__iter__` can be passed to `__getitem__`)

## Actual Result

`__iter__` produces `Cookie`s, and `__getitem__` raises `KeyError` if any non-`str` is passed

## Reproduction Steps

```python
from collections.abc import MutableMapping
import requests

# this function should work with any object that correctly implements MutableMapping
def example(some_obj):
    print(f"example(some_obj:{type(some_obj).__name__})")
    if isinstance(some_obj, MutableMapping):
        for key in some_obj:
            print(f"some_obj[{key!r}] = {some_obj[key]!r}")

example("")
example({"foo": 1})

cookie_jar = requests.cookies.RequestsCookieJar()
cookie_jar["foo"] = "1"
example(cookie_jar) # raises KeyError
```

## System Information

    $ python -m requests.help

```json
{
  "chardet": {
    "version": null
  },
  "charset_normalizer": {
    "version": "3.4.3"
  },
  "cryptography": {
    "version": ""
  },
  "idna": {
    "version": "3.11"
  },
  "implementation": {
    "name": "CPython",
    "version": "3.13.11"
  },
  "platform": {
    "release": "6.12.70",
    "system": "Linux"
  },
  "pyOpenSSL": {
    "openssl_version": "",
    "version": null
  },
  "requests": {
    "version": "2.32.5"
  },
  "system_ssl": {
    "version": "30600010"
  },
  "urllib3": {
    "version": "2.5.0"
  },
  "using_charset_normalizer": true,
  "using_pyopenssl": false
}
```

but this should apply to any version since 4d6871d9176c13affe625b1885278d396a39f21d

## Additional context

This is particularly a problem for correctly typing `RequestsCookieJar`, which is how I ran into this. See https://github.com/python/typeshed/issues/15457

title: Chardet is used, when it is available, not when `[use-chardet-on-py3]`-extra is installed
body:
First: IMHO this is related to but not a duplicate of #5871 #7222 #7219

`requests` tries to use `chardet` when it can be imported. However, IMHO it should only use it, if `requests` was installed with the `[use-chardet-on-py3]`-extra.

As reported in #7219, `requests` shows a warning, when the version of `chardet` does not fulfill the requirements. However, package managers like `uv` won't respect the version requirement of the dependency of `requests` on `chardet`, when the extra is not installed (see https://github.com/astral-sh/uv/issues/18179). If another project dependency depends on `chardet`, it will be installed, but the version of `chardet` might not fulfill the requirement of `requests`.

The same applies to `charset_normalizer` or any other optional dependency.

## Expected Result

`chardet` should not be used, when `requests` is not installed with `[use-chardet-on-py3]`-extra.

## Actual Result

`requests` tries to use `chardet` and generates a log message complaining about an incompatible version.

## Reproduction Steps

```bash
uv init
# I use fixed versions to make the example more reproducible:
uv add tox==4.44.0 requests==2.32.5

cat > test.py <<EOF
import logging

LOGGER = logging.getLogger()
LOGGER.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)

import chardet
import requests
import tox

print("tox", tox.__version__)
print("requests", requests.__version__)
print("chardet", chardet.__version__)
EOF
uv run test.py
```

#### Output:

```
.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/requests/__init__.py:113: RequestsDependencyWarning: urllib3 (2.6.3) or chardet (6.0.0.post1)/charset_normalizer (3.4.4) doesn't match a supported version!
  warnings.warn(
tox 4.44.0
requests 2.32.5
chardet 6.0.0.post1
```

## System Information

    $ python -m requests.help

```json
{
  "chardet": {
    "version": "6.0.0.post1"
  },
  "charset_normalizer": {
    "version": "3.4.4"
  },
  "cryptography": {
    "version": ""
  },
  "idna": {
    "version": "3.11"
  },
  "implementation": {
    "name": "CPython",
    "version": "3.12.3"
  },
  "platform": {
    "release": "6.17.0-14-generic",
    "system": "Linux"
  },
  "pyOpenSSL": {
    "openssl_version": "",
    "version": null
  },
  "requests": {
    "version": "2.32.5"
  },
  "system_ssl": {
    "version": "300000d0"
  },
  "urllib3": {
    "version": "2.6.3"
  },
  "using_charset_normalizer": false,
  "using_pyopenssl": false
}
```

title: Dependency warning
body:
<!-- Summary. -->

There was an update of chardet to 6.0.0.post1 yesterday. Since chardet is not a dependency of requests I did not expect problems with this. 

<!-- What happened instead. -->

requests shows a warning in logs: "RequestsDependencyWarning: urllib (2.6.3) or chardet (6.0.0.post1)/charset_normalizer (3.4.4) doesn't match a supported version!" 

## System Information

Windows 11 
Python 3.13.12 
requests 2.35.5

title: chardet 6 triggers RequestsDependencyWarning on stderr
body:
<!-- Summary. -->

chardet 6.0.0 was just released, but requests only supports < 6.

This just broke our CI with:

```
/opt/hostedtoolcache/Python/3.12.12/x64/lib/python3.12/site-packages/requests/__init__.py:113:

RequestsDependencyWarning: urllib3 (2.6.3) or chardet (6.0.0dev0)/charset_normalizer (3.4.4) doesn't match a supported version!\n"
```

I also reported that strange "dev0" to the chardet project, that shouldn't be there when just doing "pip install chardet".

CI log: https://github.com/borgbackup/borg/actions/runs/22275930673/job/64439274108?pr=9397

## Reproduction Steps

Not sure, we do not directly use requests, it is just pulled in by some stuff we use.

And something installs the latest chardet and requests stumbles over it, spilling out unexpected output on stderr.

title: TooManyRedirects exception loop when requesting FQDN URLs with a trailing dot
body:
**Describe the bug**
When making a request to a URL with a Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN) that includes a trailing dot (e.g., `https://pyropus.ca./`), `requests` gets stuck in an infinite redirect loop, eventually raising a `requests.exceptions.TooManyRedirects` exception. 

This happens because the server attempts to redirect the user to the non-FQDN version (without the dot), but `requests` seems to mishandle the Host header or the redirect state with the trailing dot, causing it to loop infinitely.

**To Reproduce**
```python
import requests

res = requests.get("https://pyropus.ca./") 
```

**Expected behavior**
The request should successfully follow the redirect to the non-FQDN URL (`https://pyropus.ca/`) and return a `200 OK` status code, just like web browsers (Chrome/Firefox) or `curl` do.

**Workaround found**
If we manually inject the Host header without the trailing dot, it bypasses the loop and returns `200`:
```python
custom_headers = {'Host': 'pyropus.ca'}
res = requests.get("https://pyropus.ca./", headers=custom_headers)
print(res.status_code)
```

**System Information**
- `requests` version: Latest
- Python version: 3.10+
- OS: Linux

**Context**
This was discovered while debugging a redirect loop issue inside the Fedora Infrastructure (Anitya project).

title: `chardet` is preferred over explicit dependency `charset_normalizer`
DMTF Redfish fails with 2.32 request module
body:
The DMTF redfish is failing with the latest Request module the issue is not seen with requests-2.31.0. 

The issue is in file requests/sessions.py, self.prepare_request(req) function where by encoding "%" url converts to "%25" while preparing request url.

Debug - 
> /usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/requests/sessions.py(564)request()
-> method=method.upper(),
(Pdb) url
'https://[FE80::5200:E6FF:FEB0:457E%eth0]/redfish/v1'

> /usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/requests/sessions.py(575)request()
-> prep = self.prepare_request(req)
(Pdb) prep.url
'https://[fe80::5200:e6ff:feb0:457e%25eth0]/redfish/v1'

title: MCP-test
body: This is an example payload for creating a GitHub issue via MCP

title: AttributeError when passing None to utility functions (get_encoding_from_headers, parse_header_links)
body:
## Summary

Several utility functions in `requests.utils` crash with `AttributeError` when passed `None` instead of returning a sensible default or raising a clear `TypeError`. This can cause unexpected crashes in application code that doesn't defensively check for `None` before calling these functions.

## Affected Functions

### 1. `get_encoding_from_headers(None)`

```python
>>> from requests.utils import get_encoding_from_headers
>>> get_encoding_from_headers(None)
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'get'
```

### 2. `parse_header_links(None)`

```python
>>> from requests.utils import parse_header_links
>>> parse_header_links(None)
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'strip'
```

## Expected Behavior

These functions should either:
1. Return a sensible default (`None` or `[]`) when passed `None`, OR
2. Raise a clear `TypeError` with a helpful message

## Actual Behavior

Both functions raise confusing `AttributeError` exceptions that don't clearly indicate the problem is with the input.

## Reproduction Steps

```python
import requests
print(f"requests version: {requests.__version__}")

from requests.utils import get_encoding_from_headers, parse_header_links

# Test 1
try:
    get_encoding_from_headers(None)
except AttributeError as e:
    print(f"get_encoding_from_headers(None) -> AttributeError: {e}")

# Test 2
try:
    parse_header_links(None)
except AttributeError as e:
    print(f"parse_header_links(None) -> AttributeError: {e}")
```

## Real-World Scenario

A developer might reasonably write code like this:

```python
# Get Link header (returns None if not present)
link_header = response.headers.get('Link')

# Parse it - crashes if header wasn't present!
links = parse_header_links(link_header)
```

This pattern is common and the crash is unexpected.

## System Information

```
$ python -m requests.help
# requests 2.32.5
# Python 3.13
# macOS
```

## Suggested Fix

Add input validation at the start of each function:

```python
def get_encoding_from_headers(headers):
    if headers is None:
        return None
    # ... rest of function

def parse_header_links(value):
    if not value:
        return []
    # ... rest of function
```

Note: `parse_header_links` already handles empty string (returns `[]`), so handling `None` the same way would be consistent.

title: `timeout` is not a true end-to-end timeout (causes confusion + bugs)
body:
### Problem
Most users assume `timeout` means **total request time**.  
In Requests, that’s not true.

What `timeout` actually does:
- single float → applies to **connect + read**
- tuple → `(connect, read)`

What it **does NOT** cover:
- DNS resolution  
- connection-pool wait  
- redirects / retries  
- SSL handshake  
- request upload  
- full response download  

**Result:** requests can run way longer than expected → subtle bugs, stuck workers, and repeated support questions.

---

### Proposal (safe, incremental)

#### 1. Docs first (immediate)
- Clearly explain float vs tuple behavior.
- Explicitly list what `timeout` does **not** include.
- Add short examples showing how to implement a *real* total timeout today.

#### 2. Explicit API (opt-in, recommended)
Add a `Timeout` object:

```

Timeout(total=None, connect=None, read=None, write=None)

````

- If `total` is set, Requests enforces a **cooperative end-to-end time budget** across:
  pool wait, DNS/connect, redirects, upload, and download.
- Existing float/tuple behavior stays unchanged (no breakage).
- Docs/examples prefer `Timeout` going forward.

---

### Semantics for `Timeout(total=...)`
- Timer starts when the request starts.
- Each phase uses `min(phase_timeout, time_left)`.
- Redirects and retries consume the **same** total budget (no free retries).
- Enforcement is cooperative:
  elapsed checks before/after phases and during read loops.
- Platform limits are documented (Unix-only signals, threads can’t be force-killed in CPython).

---

### Implementation notes (high level)
- Reuse `urllib3.util.Timeout` where possible.
- Requests orchestrates `total` across adapters, pool waits, redirects, and streaming reads.
- In `HTTPAdapter.send()`:
  - `start = monotonic()`
  - recompute `time_left` before connect/read
- For streaming responses, abort/close when total is exceeded.
- Tests to cover:
  redirects, pool waits, streaming reads, very small totals, SSL/DNS edge cases.

---

### Migration path
1. Docs update + examples (now)
2. Add `Timeout` object, document as preferred (minor release)
3. Optional: discuss deprecating ambiguous single-float usage in a future major release

---

### Minimal API sketch
```python
requests.get(url, timeout=Timeout(connect=3.05, read=27))
requests.get(url, timeout=Timeout(total=10.0))
````

---

### Open questions

* Keep float/tuple forever, or deprecate in a major release?
* Wrap/extend `urllib3.util.Timeout`, or keep a small Requests-level wrapper?
* Start with a docs-only PR, or docs + experimental implementation together?

title: Decode bytes for HTTP method names instead of builtin_str in requests/models.py
body:
<open-swe-issue-content><github-issue>
method = builtin_str(method) problem

In requests/models.py, line 441, there is this code:

```python
method = builtin_str(method)
```

This causes problems when using binary method names like `b'GET'` - it converts them to the string `"b'GET'"` instead of `"GET"`.

The fix should decode bytes to string properly instead of using builtin_str.
</github-issue>

<repository>
psf/requests
</repository>

<base-commit>
a0df2cbb10419037d11d04352b3175405ab52941
</base-commit>

Please analyze this issue and provide a fix. The fix should be minimal and focused on resolving the described problem.</open-swe-issue-content>
